case_title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. PAUL COOPER et al

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT PAUL COOPER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT IS GRANTED PROVIDED THAT THE DEFENDANT PAUL COOPER COMPLY WITH THE FOLLOWING CONDITIONS: 1) PAY IMMEDIATELY TO PLAINTIFF, SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY, THE SUM OF $1,080.00 FOR RENT THROUGH DECEMBER, 2003 (SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY IS TO VERIFY THAT THEY HAVE RECEIVED THE SUMS OF $52.00 AND $660.00); 2) DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PAY, AS A CONDITION OF THE DEFAULT BEING SET ASIDE, REASONABLE EXPENSES OF MOVING HIS BELONGINGS, PRESENTLY IN STORAGE, BACK TO HIM; PAYABLE WITHIN 3 DAYS AFTER DELIVERY OF THE MOVING EXPENSE STATEMENT; AND 3) SETTING ASIDE OF THE DEFAULT IS CONDITIONAL UPON COMPLIANCE WITH THESE CONDITIONS. ATTORNEY DROHAN TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR# 1558

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Thomas E. Drohan, Esq., Legal Assistance To The Elderly, Inc. (415) 861-4444 for Moving Party Defendant Paul Cooper's Motion To Set Aside
Paul Cooper, Defendant
Byron Clayton Thompson, Esq., Thompson Lawson LLP (510) 835-1600 For Plaintiff San Francisco Housing Authority Opposing Motion
RULING:
A R G U E D; the Court made the following orders: DEFENDANT PAUL COOPER'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT is GRANTED provided that the Defendant Paul Cooper comply with the following conditions: 1) Pay immediately to Plaintiff, San Francisco Housing Authority, the sum of $1,080.00 for rent through December, 2003 (San Francisco Housing Authority is to verify that they have received the sums of $52.00 and $660.00); 2) Defendant is required to pay, as a condition of the default being set aside, reasonable expenses of moving his belongings, presently in storage, back to him; payable within 3 days after delivery of the moving expense statement; and 3) Setting aside of the default is conditional upon compliance with these conditions. Attorney Drohan to prepare an order. (301/JLW/AC) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
